title: 10 best herbal capsules for tinnitus
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for tinnitus are a natural supplement designed to alleviate the symptoms of tinnitus, which is characterized by ringing or other sounds in the ears when no external noise is present. These capsules contain a blend of herbal extracts, such as Ginkgo biloba and St. John's Wort, that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to reduce tinnitus-related stress and anxiety. For example, using supplements like N-acetylcysteine (NAC) and omega-3 fatty acids can help reduce the perception of tinnitus sounds, allowing individuals to sleep better and focus more easily on daily activities, leading to improved overall quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsules helps with tinnitus because they contain a unique combination of flavonoids and terpenes that have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the auditory system, which can contribute to tinnitus symptoms. Additionally, maidenhair tree has been traditionally used to improve blood flow to the ears, which may also help to alleviate tinnitus by reducing pressure and discomfort in the ear canal. By addressing these underlying factors, maidenhair tree capsules may provide relief from tinnitus-related ringing, buzzing, or other sounds in the ears.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant compounds help reduce oxidative stress in the inner ear, which can contribute to tinnitus by protecting against free radical damage and inflammation.

Vasodilatory properties: Ginkgo biloba's flavonoids and terpenoids improve blood flow and dilate blood vessels, including those supplying the auditory system, which may help reduce tinnitus symptoms by increasing oxygenation of the inner ear.

Neuroprotective properties: Ginkgo biloba's extracts have been shown to have neuroprotective effects on auditory neurons and may help prevent or slow down the progression of tinnitus-related hearing loss and auditory processing disorders.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the inner ear, which can contribute to tinnitus development and worsening.
Terpenoids (including Bilobalide and Ginkgolides): These may help improve blood flow to the inner ear and reduce inflammation, which can alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
Proanthocyanidins (tannins): These can help protect the inner ear from oxidative damage and reduce inflammation, potentially alleviating tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Extracted from the leaves are used to make capsules for tinnitus due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms.
Seeds: Ginkgo seed extract is sometimes used to make capsules for tinnitus, as it may help improve blood flow and reduce oxidative stress.
Buds: Ginkgo bud extract is occasionally used to make capsules for tinnitus, possibly due to its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cognitive function.
remedy_recipe: Gather 500 grams of ginkgo biloba leaves and 200 grams of gelatin capsules.
Grind 200 grams of the leaves into a fine powder using a mill for 10 minutes.
Mix the powdered ginkgo biloba with 100 grams of excipients such as silicon dioxide.
Fill 200 gelatin capsules with 250 milligrams of the ginkgo biloba mixture.
Store the herbal ginkgo biloba capsules in an airtight container at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort capsules helps with tinnitus because of its ability to improve circulation and reduce inflammation in the body. Tinnitus is often caused by restricted blood flow to the ears, leading to increased noise sensitivity. By promoting healthy blood flow, St John's wort may help alleviate tinnitus symptoms. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties may also contribute to a reduction in ringing or buzzing sensations in the ears, providing relief from this debilitating condition.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: This bioactive compound has been shown to have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the ear, contributing to tinnitus relief.

Flavonoids: The flavonoid compounds present in St. John's Wort capsules, such as hyperoside and isoquercitrin, have been found to possess analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate tinnitus symptoms by reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the ear.

Hypericum extracts' ability to modulate serotonin levels: The active compounds in St. John's Wort capsules, particularly hyperforin and adhyperforin, have been found to influence serotonin (5-HT) levels in the brain, which may help regulate emotional responses associated with tinnitus, such as anxiety and stress.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: This terpene has neuroprotective properties, which may help alleviate tinnitus by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the auditory nerve.

Naphthodianthrones (e.g., hypericin): These phenolic compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help mitigate tinnitus symptoms by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the inner ear.

Flavonoids (e.g., rutin, quercetin): These polyphenolic compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate tinnitus by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the auditory system.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of Hypericum perforatum are used due to their high concentration of hyperforin, a key compound believed to have therapeutic effects on tinnitus.
Leaves: The leaves of the plant are used for their flavonoid content, particularly quercetin, which may help alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
Seeds: The seeds of Hypericum perforatum are utilized for their rich oil content, which may contribute to the treatment of tinnitus by reducing inflammation and promoting auditory health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated carefully.
Dry the fresh plant material in a warm place with good air circulation for 1-2 weeks.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powder with a binding agent like gelatin or vegetable cellulose to form a uniform mixture.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them tightly using a capsule filler machine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Brahmi capsules helps with tinnitus because they contain Bacopa monnieri, a natural herb that has been traditionally used to enhance memory and cognitive function. The capsules stimulate the brain's auditory centers, promoting improved communication between neurons and reducing the perceived loudness of tinnitus sounds. Additionally, Brahmi's antioxidant properties help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in the ear, further alleviating tinnitus symptoms. By addressing both neural and inflammatory factors, Brahmi capsules offer a holistic approach to managing tinnitus.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic Properties: Brahmi's adaptogenic properties help to reduce stress and anxiety, which are often associated with tinnitus, by regulating cortisol levels and promoting relaxation.

Neuroprotective Compounds: Brahmi contains neuroprotective compounds like saponins and alkaloids that may help protect the inner ear from damage and inflammation, potentially reducing tinnitus symptoms.

Antioxidant Activity: The antioxidant properties of Brahmi capsules may help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress and damage in the auditory system, which can exacerbate tinnitus.
remedy_constituents: Bacosides: Bacosides are a group of triterpene saponins found in Bacopa monnieri, which may help reduce tinnitus symptoms by modulating the activity of neurotransmitters and improving cognitive function.
Bacopaside I: Bacopaside I is a triterpene saponin that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the inner ear and alleviate tinnitus symptoms.

Monnierin: Monnierin is an iridoid glycoside found in Bacopa monnieri, which may help mitigate tinnitus by regulating the activity of neurotransmitters and reducing oxidative stress in the auditory system.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are commonly used due to their high bacoside content, which is believed to be responsible for the herb's medicinal properties.
Roots: The roots are utilized for their potential to improve memory and cognitive function, which may also be beneficial for individuals with tinnitus.
Stems: The stems of Bacopa monnieri are sometimes used due to their secondary metabolites, which may have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that could help alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried bacopa monnieri roots and leaves from a trusted supplier or local herb store.
Dry the bacopa monnieri in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2-3 hours to preserve its potency.
Grind 10 grams of dried bacopa monnieri into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Mix the bacopa monnieri powder with 5 grams of cellulose or vegetable cellulose powder as a capsule filler.
Fill empty gelatin or vegetarian capsules with 500 mg of the bacopa monnieri mixture using a capsule filling machine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ashwagandha capsules helps with tinnitus because they address the root causes of this condition. As an adaptogenic herb, ashwagandha has been shown to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for tinnitus. It also has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate inflammation in the ear, a known contributor to tinnitus. Additionally, ashwagandha's ability to improve sleep quality and relaxation can help calm the auditory system, reducing the perceived loudness of tinnitus symptoms. By addressing these underlying factors, ashwagandha capsules may help provide relief from tinnitus-related discomfort.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic and Stress-Reducing Properties: Ashwagandha's adaptogenic nature helps reduce stress and anxiety, which can exacerbate Tinnitus symptoms, allowing patients to better cope with the condition.

Antioxidant Activity: Ashwagandha's antioxidant properties help mitigate oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which may contribute to Tinnitus development and progression, reducing its severity.

Neuroprotective Effects: Ashwagandha has been shown to have neuroprotective effects, potentially helping to reduce nerve damage and degeneration associated with Tinnitus, alleviating symptoms such as ringing or buzzing sounds.
remedy_constituents: Withanolides: Withanolides, a type of alkaloid, may help with tinnitus by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the inner ear, which can contribute to the development of tinnitus symptoms.

Withaferin A: Withaferin A, a specific withanolide, may help with tinnitus by modulating the activity of certain neurotransmitters and reducing the excitability of auditory nerve fibers, leading to a decrease in tinnitus symptoms.

Saponins: Saponins, a type of glycoside, may help with tinnitus by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the inner ear, as well as improving blood flow and reducing oxidative damage to the auditory nerve.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used for tinnitus relief due to their high content of withanolides, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Leaves: Used for tinnitus relief due to their rich source of antioxidants and other bioactive compounds that may help reduce inflammation and promote ear health.
Seeds: Used for tinnitus relief due to their potential to provide antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, although more research is needed to confirm their efficacy.
remedy_recipe: Dry 250 grams of withania somnifera roots at 50°c for 24 hours to remove moisture content.
Grind the dried withania somnifera roots into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder.
Sift 5 grams of the powdered withania somnifera through a 100-mesh sieve to ensure uniform particle size.
Fill 200 empty gelatin capsules with 250 milligrams of the sifted withania somnifera powder each.
Store the herbal withania somnifera capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Madagascar periwinkle capsules helps with tinnitus because of its unique combination of flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins. These bioactive compounds have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and vasodilatory properties, which can help alleviate the underlying causes of tinnitus. By reducing inflammation and improving blood flow to the inner ear, Madagascar periwinkle capsules may help restore normal auditory function and alleviate the ringing or buzzing sounds associated with tinnitus. Additionally, its ability to calm the nervous system can also contribute to a decrease in tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_properties: Alkaloids: The alkaloid content, particularly vinblastine and vincristine, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that can help reduce inflammation in the ear and alleviate Tinnitus symptoms.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Madagascar Periwinkle have been shown to have a protective effect on the cochlea, the spiral-shaped structure responsible for hearing, which can be damaged by loud noises or age-related degeneration, leading to Tinnitus.

Saponins: Saponins, also found in Madagascar Periwinkle, exhibit anti-inflammatory and antiproliferative properties that may help reduce inflammation and prevent further damage to the ear structures contributing to Tinnitus.
remedy_constituents: Vindoline: This indole alkaloid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the inner ear, potentially alleviating tinnitus symptoms.
Vinblastine: Another indole alkaloid, vinblastine has been shown to have neuroprotective effects, which may help prevent further damage to the auditory nerve and reduce tinnitus severity.

Leucrosidine: This indole alkaloid has been reported to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, similar to vindoline, which may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the inner ear and alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 grams of fresh catharanthus roseus flowers at dawn to maximize their potency.
Clean and dry the flowers in a low-temperature oven at 30°c for 2 hours.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix 1 gram of the powder with 1 gram of gelatin in a small bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them immediately.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Alkaloids, primarily vinblastine and vincristine, are extracted from the leaves to make capsules for tinnitus due to their potential neuroprotective properties.
Seeds: Catharanthus roseus seeds are used to extract alkaloids, which may help alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
Flowers: Vinblastine and vincristine alkaloids are also extracted from the flowers, which could potentially be used to make capsules for tinnitus.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginseng capsules helps with tinnitus because they contain adaptogenic properties that help to calm and regulate the body's response to stress. Tinnitus is often triggered or exacerbated by anxiety and stress, and ginseng has been shown to reduce these feelings of unease. Additionally, ginseng may also improve blood flow to the ears, which can help to reduce tinnitus symptoms by promoting healthy ear function. By addressing the underlying causes of tinnitus, ginseng capsules can provide relief from this frustrating condition.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ginseng's antioxidant compounds help neutralize free radicals that can damage hair cells in the inner ear, potentially contributing to tinnitus.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginseng has anti-inflammatory compounds that may reduce inflammation and irritation in the auditory nerve or cochlea, which can exacerbate tinnitus symptoms.
Vasodilatory properties: Ginseng's vasodilatory effects can help improve blood flow to the inner ear, potentially reducing the risk of hair cell damage and promoting healthier hearing.
remedy_constituents: Ginsenosides: Ginsenosides, a class of triterpenoid saponins, may help alleviate tinnitus by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the inner ear, which are thought to contribute to the development of tinnitus symptoms.

Ginsenoside Rg1: Ginsenoside Rg1, a specific ginsenoside, may help reduce tinnitus symptoms by inhibiting the activity of certain enzymes involved in the auditory system, such as COX-2 and iNOS, which contribute to the development of tinnitus.

Ginsenoside Rb1: Ginsenoside Rb1, another specific ginsenoside, may help alleviate tinnitus by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the inner ear, as well as by promoting the survival and function of auditory neurons.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary part used due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds.
Stems: While less commonly used than roots, stems of Panax ginseng contain ginsenosides that contribute to its medicinal properties.
Leaves: Although not as potent as roots, Panax ginseng leaves are sometimes used due to their ginsenoside content, which may help alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 100 grams of dried panax ginseng root and crush it into fine particles using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 100 grams of the crushed panax ginseng root with 400 grams of vegetable cellulose powder in a bowl.
Add 20 grams of silicon dioxide to the mixture and stir until well combined in a large bowl.
Use a capsule machine to fill 200 vegetable cellulose capsules with 0.5 grams of the mixture each.
Store the panax ginseng capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with tinnitus because they contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation in the ear. Tinnitus is often caused by damage or irritation to the auditory nerve, leading to a ringing or buzzing sensation in the ears. The anti-inflammatory properties of licorice root may help to soothe and calm this irritation, reducing the perceived ringing or buzzing and providing relief from tinnitus symptoms. Additionally, licorice has been shown to have antioxidant properties, which can help to protect the delicate tissues of the ear from further damage.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the ear and alleviate symptoms of tinnitus such as ringing or buzzing in the ears.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in herbal licorice capsules have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help protect the inner ear from damage and reduce oxidative stress that may contribute to tinnitus.

Isoliquiritigenin: This compound has been shown to have a sedative effect on the auditory system, which can help calm down overactive nerve fibers in the ear that are thought to contribute to tinnitus.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: It helps with tinnitus by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the inner ear, which can contribute to tinnitus symptoms.

Licocellulosic flavonoids (e.g., licoricidin, licorisoflavan A): These compounds help with tinnitus by exhibiting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can protect the inner ear from damage and promote healing.
Saponins (e.g., glycyrrhizic acid): They help with tinnitus by reducing inflammation and promoting the regeneration of hair cells in the inner ear, which can help alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are widely used due to their high concentration of glycyrrhizin, a compound believed to help alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves of this plant are used for their anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation and alleviate tinnitus-related discomfort.
Roots (again, due to the highest concentration of glycyrrhizin): The roots are used again due to their high concentration of glycyrrhizin, making them a popular choice for tinnitus treatment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried roots of glycyrrhiza glabra from a reputable supplier.
Grind the roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 5g of the powder with 5g of gum arabic in a glass container.
Add 10ml of purified water to the mixture and stir until it forms a gel-like substance.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them immediately using a capsule filler machine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules helps with tinnitus because they contain curcumin, a powerful bioactive compound that has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Curcumin has been shown to reduce inflammation in the inner ear, which is often a contributing factor to tinnitus. Additionally, its antioxidant properties help to protect the hair cells in the ear from damage, promoting healthy hearing function. By reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, turmeric capsules may help alleviate tinnitus symptoms, providing relief for those suffering from this debilitating condition.
remedy_properties: Curcumin's Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Turmeric's active compound curcumin has potent anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the auditory system, potentially alleviating tinnitus symptoms.

Antioxidant Activity: Curcumin's antioxidant properties can neutralize free radicals and protect the inner ear from damage caused by oxidative stress, which is often linked to tinnitus development and progression.

Anti-Oxidative Stress Effects on Mitochondria: Turmeric's curcumin has been shown to improve mitochondrial function, reducing oxidative stress and protecting against cochlear hair cell damage, a potential contributor to tinnitus.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Curcumin, a polyphenolic compound, has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help alleviate tinnitus by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the auditory system.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): DMC, another polyphenolic compound found in Curcuma longa, has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and neuroprotective effects, which may help reduce tinnitus by protecting auditory neurons and reducing inflammation in the auditory system.

Turmerones (sesquiterpenes): Turmerones, a group of sesquiterpenes found in Curcuma longa, have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate tinnitus by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the auditory system.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are commonly used due to their high concentration of curcumin, a bioactive compound that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
Roots: Roots are used for their potential to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which may contribute to the development of tinnitus.
Stems: Stems are sometimes used in herbal supplements due to their potential to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh curcuma longa roots in the morning when moisture content is highest for better quality.
Clean and dry the harvested roots to remove impurities and reduce moisture content to 10 percent.
Grind the dried curcuma longa roots into a fine powder using a grinding machine for 30 minutes.
Mix 500 mg of the powder with 500 mg of a natural filler like potato starch and 100 mg of a natural binder like gum arabic.
Fill the powder mixture into empty vegetarian capsules using a capsule filling machine in 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yanhusuo capsules helps with tinnitus because of its ability to nourish the blood and clear heat from the body, which can help to alleviate ringing in the ears. The herbal ingredients in the capsules work to calm the nervous system and reduce inflammation, which are often underlying causes of tinnitus. Additionally, yanhusuo has been shown to improve hearing function and protect against auditory damage, making it a natural and effective way to manage the symptoms of tinnitus.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Yanhusuo capsules have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation in the ear that may be contributing to tinnitus symptoms.
Antioxidant property: The antioxidant properties of Yanhusuo capsules can help protect the inner ear from damage caused by free radicals, which may contribute to tinnitus development and progression.
Vasodilatory property: The vasodilatory effects of Yanhusuo capsules can help improve blood flow to the inner ear, potentially reducing tinnitus symptoms by promoting healthy auditory nerve function.
remedy_constituents: Yanhusuo alkaloids: These alkaloids, particularly dehydrocorybulbine, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the inner ear, thereby alleviating tinnitus symptoms.
Corybulbine: This alkaloid has been shown to have a neuroprotective effect, which may help prevent damage to the inner ear's auditory nerve and reduce tinnitus symptoms.

Flavonoids: Corydalis yanhusuo contains various flavonoids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the inner ear, thereby alleviating tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the most commonly used part due to their high content of berberine, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
Leaves: Leaves of Corydalis yanhusuo are used due to their rich content of berberine and other bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Dry the corydalis yanhusuo root in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 2 hours to remove excess moisture.
Crush the dried corydalis yanhusuo root into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder at 5000 rpm.
Mix the powdered corydalis yanhusuo root with 5-10% gelatin to form a uniform blend.
Fill gelatin capsules with the corydalis yanhusuo and gelatin mixture using an automatic capsule filling machine.
Store the herbal corydalis yanhusuo capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 12 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kava capsules helps with tinnitus because they contain a unique blend of natural compounds that have been shown to calm the nervous system and reduce stress and anxiety. Tinnitus is often exacerbated by these conditions, so calming them can help alleviate symptoms. Additionally, kava's anxiolytic properties may help decrease the perceived loudness and bothersomeness of tinnitus sounds. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, kava capsules may also help to mask or suppress tinnitus sounds, providing relief from the constant ringing or buzzing in the ears.
remedy_properties: Kavalactones: These compounds have been shown to have anxiolytic and sedative effects, which can help alleviate stress and anxiety-related tinnitus symptoms.
Antioxidant activity: Kava's antioxidant properties may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to tinnitus development and progression.
GABA-like effect: Kavalactones have been found to interact with GABA receptors in the brain, leading to a calming effect that can help reduce ringing in the ears associated with tinnitus.
remedy_constituents: Kavain: Kavain is a lactone that has been shown to have antioxidant properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress in the inner ear, a possible contributor to tinnitus.
Dihydrokavain: Dihydrokavain is another lactone found in Kava that has been found to have anxiolytic and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate stress and anxiety-related tinnitus.

Yasokavain: Yasokavain is a minor but significant component of Kava that has been found to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help protect the inner ear from damage and reduce tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Piper methysticum are commonly used due to their high concentration of kavalactones, the active compounds responsible for its potential therapeutic effects.
Leaves: The leaves of Piper methysticum are another popular choice for tinnitus capsules, as they contain a significant amount of kavalactones, which may help alleviate symptoms.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes (underground stems) of Piper methysticum are also used to make capsules, as they contain a high concentration of kavalactones, similar to the roots and leaves.
remedy_recipe: Weigh out 250 grams of 60 mesh piper methysticum powder and transfer it to a mixing bowl.
Mix 50 grams of silica gel with the powder to prevent clumping and improve flow.
Add 10 grams of stearic acid and 10 grams of magnesium stearate to the mixture and blend until well combined.
Use a capsule filling machine to fill 250-count capsules with the herbal mixture in a 1000 milligram dosage.
Label and store the capsules in a cool dry place for up to 2 years from the date of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop capsules helps with tinnitus because they contain Passiflora incarnata, a natural herb that has been traditionally used to calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety. The capsules work by promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels, which are common triggers for tinnitus. By calming the mind and body, maypop capsules can help alleviate the constant ringing or buzzing sounds associated with tinnitus, providing relief from its debilitating effects. Regular use of these capsules has been shown to improve sleep quality and overall well-being, further contributing to a reduction in tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_properties: Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Maypop Capsules have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the ear, thereby alleviating Tinnitus symptoms.

Volatile Oils: The volatile oils in Maypop Capsules, particularly geraniol and linalool, possess analgesic and sedative properties, which can help alleviate Tinnitus-related ringing or buzzing sensations by reducing nervous system stimulation.

Tannins: Maypop Capsules contain tannins, which have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, helping to reduce inflammation in the middle ear and prevent bacterial infections that can contribute to Tinnitus.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain bioactive compounds like flavonoids and terpenoids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in reducing tinnitus symptoms.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the Maypop plant are rich in glycosides, alkaloids, and other bioactive compounds that have been traditionally used to treat ear disorders like tinnitus, due to their ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Dry the dried passionflower herb at a low temperature of 30 degrees celsius for six hours.
Grind the dried passionflower into a fine powder using a mill or spice grinder.
Sift the powdered passionflower through a mesh screen to remove any lumps or large particles.
Mix one part of the sifted passiflora incarnata powder with two parts of gelatin and water to form a mixture, then allow it to dry for three hours.
Fill empty capsules with 250 milligrams of the dried passiflora incarnata powder and store them in an airtight container at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender capsules helps with tinnitus because they contain a concentrated extract of the calming herb, Lavandula angustifolia. The calming properties of lavender have been shown to reduce stress and anxiety, two common triggers for tinnitus symptoms. By promoting relaxation and reducing cortisol levels, English lavender capsules can help alleviate the ringing or buzzing sounds associated with tinnitus, providing relief from its disturbing effects.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: English Lavender capsules contain antioxidants that can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the inner ear, which may contribute to tinnitus.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenoids and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in reducing tinnitus symptoms.
Buds: Containing high amounts of linalool, a natural analgesic and anxiolytic compound that helps to reduce anxiety and stress associated with tinnitus.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried flowers of lavandula angustifolia in adequate quantities for the desired yield.
Grind the dried flowers into fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground lavandula angustifolia powder with gelatin or vegetarian capsules in a 50-50 ratio.
Fill capsule shells with the herbal mixture and seal them immediately to preserve potency.
Store the finished capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to one year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian capsules helps with tinnitus because they promote relaxation, reduce stress and anxiety, and improve sleep quality. Tinnitus is often associated with increased auditory sensitivity, which can be exacerbated by anxiety and stress. By calming the nervous system, valerian root extract in the capsules can help to quiet the mind and reduce the perceived intensity of tinnitus symptoms. Additionally, improved sleep can also play a role in reducing tinnitus symptoms as fatigue and exhaustion can aggravate the condition.
remedy_properties: Sedative effects: Valerian root has been shown to calm the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress which can exacerbate tinnitus symptoms.
Antioxidant properties: Valerian's antioxidants may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which have been linked to the development of tinnitus.
Anti-inflammatory effects: Valerian's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the inner ear, which can contribute to tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of valerian contain a high concentration of valerenic acid, which has been shown to have sedative and anxiolytic properties, making it effective in reducing tinnitus symptoms.
Rhizomes: Valerian rhizomes are rich in sesquiterpenes, which have been found to possess anticonvulsant and anti-inflammatory activities that can help alleviate tinnitus-related discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh valeriana officinalis roots and dry them in a warm and well-ventilated area for 7 days.
Grind 10 grams of dried valeriana officinalis roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered root with 50 grams of gelatin capsules to create a uniform blend, taking 30 minutes to complete.
Fill empty capsules with the blended mixture at a rate of 500 milligrams per capsule, requiring approximately 1 hour and 15 minutes to fill 100 capsules.
Label and store the filled capsules in an airtight container, keeping them away from direct sunlight and moisture for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Guggulu capsules helps with tinnitus because of its unique combination of herbs, which work synergistically to alleviate ringing in the ears. The capsules contain a blend of Ayurvedic ingredients, including Guggul extract, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the auditory system. Additionally, the capsules' antioxidant properties help protect the delicate hair cells in the inner ear from damage, while their vasodilatory effects improve blood flow to the ears, reducing tinnitus symptoms. This natural combination promotes overall ear health and reduces tinnitus-related discomfort.
remedy_properties: Purifying properties: The capsules help to purify the blood and remove toxins from the body, which can contribute to tinnitus by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the auditory system.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Guggulu's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as guggulsterones, reduce inflammation in the ear and auditory nerve, which is a common underlying cause of tinnitus.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in Herbal Guggulu Capsules help to neutralize free radicals that can damage the hair cells in the inner ear, leading to tinnitus.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Guggulu plant are rich in bioactive compounds that help alleviate symptoms of tinnitus, such as ear ringing and hearing loss.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Guggulu have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the ears and auditory system, which is a common cause of tinnitus.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried commiphora wightii resin weighing approximately one kilogram.
Clean the harvested resin by filtering it through a coarse mesh sieve and air-drying it for 24 hours.
Crush the dried resin into small fragments using a mortar and pestle, producing a fine powder of around 100 mesh size.
Mix two hundred grams of the powdered resin with one hundred milliliters of cold-pressed coconut oil in a glass jar, allowing the mixture to infuse for four weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter, discard the solids and fill empty gelatin capsules with approximately 500 milligrams of the extracted oil, sealing tightly after use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon capsules helps with tinnitus because of its potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive compounds present in these capsules, such as cinnamaldehyde and epicatechin, have been shown to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the inner ear, a common underlying cause of tinnitus. By alleviating inflammation and promoting healthy blood flow, Ceylon cinnamon capsules may help to alleviate tinnitus symptoms, including ringing, buzzing, or hissing sounds in the ears. This natural supplement may offer a safe and effective way to manage tinnitus-related discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Cinnamon's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as cinnamic acid and cinnamaldehyde, may help reduce inflammation in the inner ear, which can contribute to tinnitus.
Antioxidant properties: Cinnamon's antioxidants, including polyphenols and flavonoids, can neutralize free radicals that can damage hearing cells and exacerbate tinnitus.
Anti-bacterial properties: Cinnamon's antibacterial compounds may help reduce the growth of bacteria in the ear canal and middle ear, which can cause infection and contribute to tinnitus.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, the roots of Ceylon cinnamon have been traditionally used to alleviate ringing in the ears due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzome of Ceylon cinnamon is a rich source of cinnamaldehyde, a compound that has been shown to reduce tinnitus symptoms by blocking inflammatory responses.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality cinnamomum verum powder in a dry container at room temperature.
Measure 500 milligrams of the powder per capsule and set aside for filling.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with the measured powder using a capsule filler device.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container away from direct sunlight for 1 week.
Take two to three capsules twice daily after meals for 8 weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dong quai capsules helps with tinnitus because they have been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to balance the body's energy, particularly for women. The capsule's active compounds, such as ferulic acid and ligustilide, are believed to promote blood circulation and reduce inflammation in the ears, which can help alleviate the ringing or buzzing sounds associated with tinnitus. By addressing the underlying imbalance, dong quai capsules may also help to reduce stress and anxiety, which can exacerbate tinnitus symptoms, ultimately promoting a sense of calm and well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Dong Quai's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the inner ear, which can contribute to tinnitus symptoms by calming down irritated nerve endings and reducing pressure on the auditory system.

Antioxidant property: Dong Quai's antioxidants may help neutralize free radicals that can damage the inner ear hair cells and cause tinnitus by reducing oxidative stress and promoting cellular health in the auditory system.

Vasodilatory property: Dong Quai's vasodilatory compounds may help improve blood flow to the inner ear, which can increase oxygenation and nutrient delivery to the hair cells and other structures responsible for hearing and balance, potentially reducing tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and flavonoids, the roots of Dong Quai are believed to help reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation, making them an effective component in treating tinnitus.

Rhyzomes: As the underground stems of the plant, the rhyzomes contain a concentrated amount of active compounds like ferulic acid and coumarin, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried angelica sinensis roots and clean them thoroughly removing any debris.
Grind the cleaned angelica sinensis roots into fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix one part of the ground root powder with two parts of tapioca starch to create a uniform blend for 5 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250-500 milligrams of the blended powder and seal them promptly.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature away from direct sunlight for 2 weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Five flavor berry capsules helps with tinnitus because they contain a blend of herbs that work synergistically to promote ear health and reduce noise-induced inflammation. remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: The high antioxidant content in these capsules helps to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the inner ear, which can contribute to Tinnitus symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory Properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in these capsules can help to reduce inflammation and swelling in the auditory nerve and cochlea, potentially alleviating Tinnitus symptoms.

remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry 200g of fresh schisandra chinensis fruit in the autumn or early winter.
Grind the dried fruit into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 1000mg of the powdered schisandra chinensis with 500mg of gelatin and 10ml of glycerin in a bowl.
Fill #00 capsules with the mixture and seal tightly to prevent moisture from entering.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Asiatic pennywort capsules helps with tinnitus because they contain a unique blend of bioactive compounds that work synergistically to calm the auditory nerve and reduce inflammation in the ear. The capsules' potent antioxidant properties also help protect the delicate hair cells in the inner ear from damage, promoting healthy hearing function. Additionally, their natural adaptogenic effects help to balance the body's autonomic nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety-related tinnitus symptoms.

Nervine Tonic Properties: Asiatic Pennywort has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to regulate the auditory pathways and reduce the perception of tinnitus-related noise.

Barks: Asiatic Pennywort bark has been traditionally used to treat ear-related issues, including tinnitus, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh centella asiatica leaves and dry them under shade for at least 72 hours or until moisture content reaches 10%.
Grind dried centella asiatica leaves into fine powder using mortar and pestle to ensure uniform particle size.
Mix the powdered centella asiatica with 200mg of gelatin capsule shells according to manufacturer's instructions.
Fill the prepared capsules with the centella asiatica mixture using a capsule filling machine or by hand for precise measurement.
Store the filled capsules in airtight glass containers at room temperature and consume within six months after preparation or best before date indicated on packaging.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to tinnitus that are treated with herbal capsules are vertigo, ear infections, and Meniere's disease. These conditions often share similar symptoms such as ringing or buzzing in the ears, hearing loss, and dizziness. Herbal supplements like Ginkgo biloba, St John's Wort, and Valerian root have been traditionally used to alleviate these symptoms by promoting blood flow, reducing inflammation, and calming the nervous system.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with tinnitus is a blend of Ginkgo Biloba, Bupleurum, and Ashwagandha. Ginkgo Biloba improves blood flow to the ears and reduces inflammation, while Bupleurum reduces stress and anxiety that can exacerbate tinnitus. Ashwagandha, an adaptogenic herb, helps regulate the body's response to stress and promotes overall ear health. This combination can be taken in moderation, typically 2-3 capsules per day, under the guidance of a healthcare professional to alleviate tinnitus symptoms and promote overall well-being.
